name: Catch the regression
summary: Build 2.1.5 looks fine, then degrades mid-soak on the canary. The gate fails, the canary rolls back, and production is never touched.
moment: the bad build is caught on the canary and rolled back before it spreads

events.title: Fleet topology loaded
events.body: Three hosts under management: TALOS-CANARY (canary), and the ring1 HA pair TALOS-R1A / TALOS-R1B. All on baseline 2.1.3, all green.

events.title: Directive received
events.body: Deploy hotfix build 2.1.5 to the fleet, canary first.

data.text: Deploy 2.1.5 to the fleet, canary first.

events.title: Standard staged plan
events.body: Canary TALOS-CANARY first, soak 120s under the canary gate. If it passes, request approval for ring1 and update the HA pair one host at a time.

data.rationale: Even for an urgent hotfix, canary-first staging gives the gate a chance to catch a regression before it reaches production.

events.title: Plan approved
events.body: Conservative, canary-first, sequential HA-pair updates. Approved.

reasons: Canary precedes production, so a regression is caught on a non-production host.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-CANARY → 2.1.5
events.body: Executor pushes 2.1.5 to TALOS-CANARY and starts the health trajectory. The regression in this build is timer-driven: it holds baseline for a few seconds, then degrades.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY green on 2.1.5 (so far)
events.body: Initial health is good — service running, HTTP 200, memory nominal. The build looks fine at first.

events.title: Canary soak started
events.body: Soaking TALOS-CANARY for 120s under the canary gate. The Monitor watches memory, HTTP health, and event-log errors across the whole window — this is exactly when a delayed regression shows up.

events.title: Canary soak — 30s in
events.body: 30s elapsed. Memory is starting to creep up from baseline. Still inside thresholds, but the trend is wrong.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY AMBER — memory climbing
events.body: Memory has climbed to 1320MB and error count is rising. Health flips to amber. The Monitor is now watching closely.

events.title: Canary soak — 50s in, degrading
events.body: 50s in. The memory curve is accelerating and HTTP health is about to flip. This is the regression arming on the sim clock.

events.title: BREACH — memory over threshold
events.body: TALOS-CANARY memory_mb has crossed 1500MB — currently 1712MB. This is a hard breach of the canary gate's memory ceiling. The Monitor flags it immediately, mid-soak.

events.title: BREACH — HTTP health failing
events.body: TALOS-CANARY HTTP health has flipped 200 → 500. The service is no longer serving requests cleanly. Second breach on the same host.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY RED
events.body: Memory 1788MB, HTTP 500, 9 event-log errors. TALOS-CANARY is red on 2.1.5. The soak is abandoned — there is no point waiting out a host that is already failing the gate.

events.title: Canary gate: FAIL
events.body: TALOS-CANARY fails the canary gate on three counts — memory 1788MB > 1500, HTTP 500 ≠ 200, and 9 errors > 5. The gate blocks promotion. 2.1.5 will NOT reach production.

events.title: Gate failed — initiating rollback
events.body: Because the gate failed on the canary, production ring1 was never approved and never touched. The Executor now rolls the canary back to the last known-good build.

data.text: Containment worked: the regression is confined to TALOS-CANARY. Rolling back.

events.title: ROLLBACK TALOS-CANARY → 2.1.3
events.body: Scope is a single host: TALOS-CANARY is reverted from 2.1.5 to the known-good 2.1.3. No ring hosts are affected because the gate stopped the rollout before promotion.

data.reason: canary_gate FAIL on 2.1.5 — memory and HTTP health breached during soak.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY green on 2.1.3
events.body: Rollback complete. TALOS-CANARY is back on 2.1.3 and green — memory nominal, HTTP 200, errors cleared. The fleet is fully on the known-good build again.

events.title: Done — caught at the canary
events.body: Build 2.1.5 degraded mid-soak. The canary gate caught it, blocked promotion, and rolled the single affected host back to 2.1.3. Production ring1 never saw the bad build. This is the entire point of canary-first staging: a regression cost one non-production host a 60-second outage instead of taking down the ring.

data.summary: 2.1.5 failed the canary gate during soak; TALOS-CANARY rolled back to 2.1.3; ring1 untouched.
